typecheck.command: cd quant-dashboard && python3 -m mypy app/ src/ --ignore-missing-imports
typecheck.detail: Success: no issues found in 23 source files

lint.command: cd quant-dashboard && python3 -m ruff check src/ app/
lint.detail: 11 errors found (9 fixable): 10 pre-existing whitespace issues (W291/W293) in app/api/backtest.py and app/main.py, plus 1 line-length (E501) in app/api/backtest.py. All cosmetic and auto-fixable. Same pre-existing issues from prior milestones. Not blocking.

appliedUpdates.description: Added 'Custom Static HTML Pages' section to frequi-compat.md documenting the pattern for serving non-FreqUI HTML files: serve from committed source paths (e.g., frontend/benchmark.html) not from frontend/dist/ which is gitignored and regenerated by FreqUI builds. Define explicit FastAPI routes BEFORE static file mounts.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: Custom non-FreqUI HTML pages must be served via explicit FastAPI routes defined BEFORE the static file mount and SPA fallback. Files must be served from committed paths (e.g., frontend/custom-pages/ or frontend/), NOT from frontend/dist/ which is gitignored and regenerated by FreqUI builds.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature benchmark-visual-overlay served benchmark.html from frontend/dist/ (stale build artifact), causing a deployment-breaking bug fixed by fix-benchmark-page-path. The pattern of adding custom pages alongside FreqUI may recur and needs normative guidance.

rejectedObservations.observation: services.yaml has no gaps — worker used existing configuration correctly
rejectedObservations.reason: No action needed; confirmed existing config is adequate.

rejectedObservations.observation: frequi-compat.md should document custom page serving pattern
rejectedObservations.reason: Applied directly to library (see appliedUpdates) — not rejected, but triaged to 'apply now'.
